title: Scalar Lax-Friedrichs update is L-infinity stable under the CFL condition

source.citation: Randall J. LeVeque, Finite Volume Methods for Hyperbolic Problems, Cambridge University Press, 2002, Sec. 12.5, p. 232.
source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: For the scalar Lax-Friedrichs update uNext = ((1 + cfl) / 2) uLeft + ((1 - cfl) / 2) uRight, if -1 <= cfl <= 1 and both |uLeft| and |uRight| are bounded by M, then |uNext| is also bounded by M.
informal.latex: Let \(\lambda\in\mathbb{R}\) satisfy \(-1\le\lambda\le1\). Define \(u^{+}=((1+\lambda)/2)u_L+((1-\lambda)/2)u_R\). If \(|u_L|\le M\) and \(|u_R|\le M\), then \(|u^{+}|\le M\).
assumptions: cfl, uLeft, uRight, and M are real numbers
informal.expected_conclusion: The scalar Lax-Friedrichs update remains bounded by M in absolute value.

declarations.name: laxFriedrichsStep
declarations.statement: def laxFriedrichsStep (cfl uLeft uRight : ℝ) : ℝ := laxFriedrichsLeftWeight cfl * uLeft + laxFriedrichsRightWeight cfl * uRight

declarations.name: laxFriedrichsStep_abs_le
declarations.statement: theorem laxFriedrichsStep_abs_le (cfl uLeft uRight M : ℝ) (hcflLower : -1 ≤ cfl) (hcflUpper : cfl ≤ 1) (hLeft : |uLeft| ≤ M) (hRight : |uRight| ≤ M) : |laxFriedrichsStep cfl uLeft uRight| ≤ M

tasks.input: Translate the scalar Lax-Friedrichs CFL L-infinity stability statement into Lean 4 using laxFriedrichsStep.
tasks.target: theorem laxFriedrichsStep_abs_le (cfl uLeft uRight M : ℝ) (hcflLower : -1 ≤ cfl) (hcflUpper : cfl ≤ 1) (hLeft : |uLeft| ≤ M) (hRight : |uRight| ≤ M) : |laxFriedrichsStep cfl uLeft uRight| ≤ M

tasks.input: Given the Lax-Friedrichs step definition and theorem statement, complete the Lean 4 proof.
tasks.target: A proof using weight nonnegativity, abs_add_le, abs_mul, mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left, linarith, and ring.

tasks.input: Find the LeanNumerics theorem needed to prove Lax-Friedrichs scalar CFL stability.
